notes: Batch of 10 Events listings: fills Óbuda, Újbuda, Belváros gaps and expands Events category with official venues.
missingOrUncertain: Several venues use dynamic or show-specific pricing; pricePerClass is a planning figure or 0 where only ranges exist on official pages.
rating and reviewCount are 0 unless explicitly stated on official sources.
Budapest Park postal address is 1095 Fábián Juli tér but the open-air venue is on Hajógyári-sziget — mapped to borough Óbuda / neighborhood Óbuda Island.
MOM Kulturális Központ (1124 Csörsz utca) mapped to Újbuda / Infopark as closest canonical district.
OKK listing image sourced from kulturkozpont.jegy.hu (official ticket portal) for a current theatre production.

document.name: Budapest Park
document.shortDescription: Open-air concert and party park on Hajógyári-sziget — main stage, terraces, and summer festival season.
document.longDescription:
Budapest Park is Budapest's seasonal open-air venue on Hajógyári-sziget (Shipyard Island), open roughly April through early October with concerts, DJ nights, and food vendors. The site lists gate times per show, cloakroom fees (499 HUF), and transport by tram, suburban rail H6/H7, and bus. Buy tickets on budapestpark.hu.

Sources: https://www.budapestpark.hu/en/park

document.name: Hungarian National Gallery
document.shortDescription: Hungary's national art museum in Buda Castle — permanent collections, dome views, and rotating exhibitions.
document.longDescription:
The Hungarian National Gallery (Magyar Nemzeti Galéria) occupies the Buda Castle complex at Szent György tér 2. General opening hours are Tuesday–Sunday 10:00–18:00 (ticket office until 17:00; last entry 17:00). The dome may open depending on weather. Check en.mng.hu for current exhibitions and ticket options.

Sources: https://en.mng.hu/

document.name: Akvárium Klub
document.shortDescription: Central Pest club under Erzsébet tér — indoor hall and garden gigs steps from Deák Ferenc tér.
document.longDescription:
Akvárium Klub is a long-running concert and club venue at Erzsébet tér 12 near Deák Ferenc tér. The program mixes Hungarian and international live acts and club nights in the main hall and garden areas. Check akvariumklub.hu for the calendar and tickets.

Sources: https://akvariumklub.hu/

document.name: Fonó Budai Zene Ház
document.shortDescription: Roots, folk, and jazz club in Újbuda — nightly concerts and the annual Budapest Folk Festival hub.
document.longDescription:
Fonó Budai Zene Ház at Sztregova utca 3 is one of Budapest's key venues for folk, world, and jazz, including the Budapest Folk Festival program. See fono.hu for concert dates, ticket prices, and table reservations.

Sources: https://fono.hu/

document.name: Trafó House of Contemporary Arts
document.shortDescription: Contemporary theatre, dance, and music in a former transformer station near Rákóczi tér.
document.longDescription:
Trafó Kortárs Művészeti Központ (Trafó House of Contemporary Arts) at Liliom utca 41 presents experimental theatre, dance, concerts, and DJ programs. Browse trafo.hu for the season program and tickets.

Sources: https://trafo.hu/

document.name: Hungarian State Opera
document.shortDescription: Neo-Renaissance opera house on Andrássy út — opera, ballet, and guided building tours.
document.longDescription:
The Hungarian State Opera (Magyar Állami Operaház) at Andrássy út 22 is the historic home of opera and ballet in Budapest. Book performances and building tours through opera.hu.

Sources: https://www.opera.hu/

document.name: MOM Cultural Centre
document.shortDescription: Bauhaus cultural centre on the Buda hills — theatre, concerts, and courtyard events.
document.longDescription:
MOM Kulturális Központ (MOM Cultural Centre) at Csörsz utca 18 is a protected Bauhaus building with a 650-seat theatre, dome room, and outdoor stage. Ticket office hours and programs are listed on momkult.hu; email jegy@momkult.hu for tickets.

Sources: https://momkult.hu/

document.name: Barba Negra Live Club
document.shortDescription: Large-capacity concert hall by the Danube — rock, metal, and pop tours with listed tickets from 3,990 HUF.
document.longDescription:
Barba Negra Live Club at Neumann János utca 4 hosts major Hungarian and international concert tours. The public program lists ticket prices per show (many from 3,990 HUF upward on barbanegra.hu). pricePerClass uses the lowest listed entry price as a planning figure.

Sources: https://www.barbanegra.hu/

document.name: Óbuda Cultural Centre
document.shortDescription: District cultural centre in Óbuda — theatre, concerts, and community programs across local venues.
document.longDescription:
Óbudai Kulturális Központ (Óbuda Cultural Centre) at San Marco utca 81 programmes theatre, music, and family events across Óbuda and partner houses. Tickets via kulturkozpont.hu and kulturkozpont.jegy.hu; office phone (06-1) 388-7370 on weekdays 9:00–17:00.

Sources: https://kulturkozpont.hu/ https://kulturkozpont.jegy.hu/

document.name: MÜPA Budapest
document.shortDescription: Palace of Arts on the Danube — Béla Bartók National Concert Hall, Ludwig Museum, and festival programs.
document.longDescription:
MÜPA Budapest (Palace of Arts) at Komor Marcell utca 1 hosts classical, jazz, world music, dance, and contemporary programs in the Bartók National Concert Hall and other halls, plus the Ludwig Museum. Browse mupa.hu for tickets and schedules.

Sources: https://www.mupa.hu/en
